sanskrit:
मां च योऽव्यभिचारेण भक्तियोगेन सेवते।
स गुणान्समतीत्यैतान्ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते॥
transliteration:
māṃ ca yo 'vyabhicāreṇa bhakti-yogena sevate
sa guṇān samatītyaitān brahma-bhūyāya kalpate
literal_meaning: One who serves Me with unfailing devotion (bhakti-yoga) transcends the three guṇas and becomes fit for the Brahman state.
contextual_meaning: Unwavering devotion to the Self (Kṛṣṇa) is the fast-track to psychological freedom; by keeping attention anchored in the sacred core, the mind exits the endless tug-of-war of sattva, rajas and tamas.
psychological_state: Secure, single-pointed focus that overrides mood swings and identity fluctuations.
trigger_scenario: A recovering perfectionist who keeps relapsing into anxiety; she installs a daily ritual of five minutes of heartfelt mantra before work and notices the urge to over-check reports fades.
anchor_text: Hold steady devotion to the sacred core within.
